channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli airstrikes kill at least 5 in Gaza, including 2 babies, Palestinians say
item.description: Palestinian medics say Israeli airstrikes have killed at least five people in the Gaza Strip, including two infants and a woman.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hostages in Gaza endure another winter as their families plead for a ceasefire
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — When Luis Har was kidnapped by Hamas-led militants on the warm morning of Oct. 7, 2023, he was forced into Gaza wearing shorts and a T-shirt.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader says his son-in-law has been kidnapped in Caracas
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González, who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election, said his son-in-law was kidnapped Tuesday in Venezuela's capital.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What would Bonhoeffer do? Anti-Nazi pastor's legacy claimed, debated across political spectrum
item.description: Shortly before he was hanged by the Nazis in 1945 at age 39, Dietrich Bonhoeffer reportedly told a fellow prisoner: “This is the end — for me the beginning of life.” It was – more than he knew.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India confirms first HMPV cases amid surge in China
item.description: India confirmed its first cases of human metapneumovirus (HMPV) infections in children, with a total of seven cases reported as of Tuesday. The cases were reported in Bengaluru, Nagpur, Tamil Nadu and Ahmedabad, according to The Economic Times.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Why Greenland? Remote but resource-rich island occupies a key position in a warming world
item.description: Greenland plays an outsized role in the daily weather experienced by billions of people and in the climate changes taking shape all over the planet.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Canada's Liberal Party in search of a new leader while dealing with Trump threats, pending election
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's Liberal Party finds itself in search of a new leader while dealing with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump's threats to impose steep tariffs on Canadian goods and with Canada's election just months away.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump repeated his warnings that all hell will break loose in the Middle East if hostages kidnapped from Israel and held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel's military launches a wave of raids across the occupied West Bank
item.description: The Israeli military launched a wave of deadly raids across the occupied West Bank overnight and into Tuesday, killing at least three Palestinians who it said were militants. Hamas said in a statement that one of its veteran commanders, Jaafar Dababsah, was killed by Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France marks 10 years since deadly Charlie Hebdo attack
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A decade after Islamic extremist gunmen stormed the offices of the satirical newspaper Charlie Hebdo in a deadly assault that shook France to its core and ignited a global outcry in defense of freedom of speech, the nation paused on Tuesday to honor the victims and renew its resolve to
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangladesh's ailing former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia to undergo medical treatment in London
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Bangladesh’s ailing former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia was traveling to London on Tuesday for medical treatment, her aides said, leaving behind a nation grappling with uncertainty over its political future after her archrival, former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina, was ousted i
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. arrives in Greenland amid father's interest in acquiring island
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. has arrived in Greenland amid his father’s interest in acquiring the island. “Don Jr. and my Reps landing in Greenland. The reception has been great. They, and the Free World, need safety, security, strength, and PEACE! This is a deal that must happen. MAGA.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Senegal and Chad say ousting of French troops was their sovereign decision
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Senegalese and Chadian leaders said that the ousting of French troops from their territories was their sovereign decision, rejecting remarks from French President Emmanuel Macron that suggested otherwise.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and BABACAR DIONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Why more frequent cold blasts could be coming from global warming
item.description: Frigid air that normally stays trapped in the Arctic has escaped, plunging deep into the United States for an extended visit that is expected to provoke teeth-chattering but not be record-shattering.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean anti-corruption agency receives new court warrant to detain impeached President Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s anti-corruption agency said it received a new court warrant on Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after its previous attempt was blocked by the presidential security service last week.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea says it tested hypersonic intermediate range missile aimed at remote Pacific targets
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Tuesday its latest weapons test was a new hypersonic intermediate-range missile designed to strike remote targets in the Pacific as leader Kim Jong Un vowed to further expand his collection of nuclear-capable weapons to counter rival nations.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 9 miners are trapped in a coal mine in India's northeastern Assam state
item.description: At least nine workers are trapped inside a flooded coal mine in India’s northeastern Assam state and authorities have summoned the army to help in the rescue operation.
item.dc:creator: Wasbir Hussain, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Law student killed by elephant during vacation to Thailand: officials
item.description: Blanca Ojanguren, a 22-year-old student from Spain, was killed by an elephant on Friday during a bathing session at Koh Yao Elephant Care
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurdles remain as Israel and Hamas once again inch toward a ceasefire deal
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Hamas once again appear to be inching toward a ceasefire that could wind down the 15-month war in Gaza and bring home dozens of Israelis held hostage there. Both Israel and Hamas are under pressure from outgoing U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congo to execute over 170 people convicted of armed robbery, official says
item.description: Over 170 inmates have been flown from the Democratic Republic of the Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to a high-security prison where they will be executed, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Pierre Poilievre? Canada's Conservative leader seeking to become next prime minister after Trudeau exit
item.description: The leader of Canada's Conservative party, Pierre Poilievre, has been leading in the polls and is favorite to become the country's next prime minister following the exit of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau announces resignation following party pressure amid criticisms of Trump, budget handling
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced plans to resign after calls increased for his ouster over his handling of economic policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 killed in West Bank 'shooting spree' including Israeli police officer: reports
item.description: A shooting spree by Palestinian terrorists in the West Bank has left three Israelis dead as a manhunt is ongoing for the suspects.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea carries out first missile launch test since Trump's election
item.description: North Korea launched its first missile test since Election Day on Monday, strongly implying that it does not plan to play nice with President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German Christmas market attack victim dies at hospital weeks later, increasing death toll to 6
item.description: A woman who was injured during the Dec. 20 attack at a German Christmas market died at a hospital on Monday, increasing the death toll to six.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the siege outside South Korea's presidential compound
item.description: A standoff between rival government forces outside the presidential compound in South Korea is a startling development, even for observers used to the country’s famously rough and tumble politics.
item.dc:creator: Foster Klug, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to announce resignation: Reports
item.description: It remains unclear whether Justin Trudeau will leave immediately or stay on as prime minister until a new leader is selected, the report added.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Austrian nationalist party leader rumored to be in talks to form government
item.description: Herbert Kickl, leader of Austria's right-wing Freedom Party, met with President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday, further fueling rumors that he will be asked to form a government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli PM office denies reports that Hamas forwarded list of hostages to release in event of deal
item.description: Israel denied reports that Hamas forwarded a list of 34 hostages it could release in exchange for a cease-fire Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9
